note: Query this registry before creating a new component (anti-bloat rule). All backwards are hand-derived and FD-verified; there is no autograd in this crate.

components.name: MeshTensor / MeshTopology
components.purpose: The hypergraph latent space AS a mesh tensor (contraction, NOT inversion). The signed-hypergraph incidence (cycles/signs/scale) is the MESH (MeshTopology = the operator side); the latent is a TENSOR FIELD on it -- a d-vector per node and per hyperedge (MeshTensor = the state side). Computation is CONTRACTION along the mesh (node<->edge), delegating to hg_message. The on-mission hypergraph<->tensor conjugation and the substrate a holonomy embedding (HSiKAN / Gomb-Soma) + holonomy feedback act on. Replaces the flat-vector/precision-matrix mis-framing of the (frozen) evolvent line.
components.source_experiment: hypergraph-latent-as-mesh-tensor (2026-07-16, user directive: 'use hypergraph latent space as a mesh tensor instead')
supported_tasks: hypergraph latent as a mesh tensor field (contraction substrate)
signed-HGNN conv round node->edge->node with FD-verified backward
the field HSiKAN / Gomb-Soma / holonomy feedback operate on
components.observation_schema: MeshTopology(cycles (n_edges,k), signs, scale (n_nodes), n_nodes, k); MeshTensor node field (n_nodes,d)
components.output_schema: node/edge tensor fields (contracted); conv_round -> (n_nodes,d); conv_round_backward -> grad on input nodes (composed hg_message backwards, FD-verified)
known_limitations: thin typed wrapper over hg_message contraction -- the contraction is linear (signed mean / signed sum); nonlinear per-edge maps (HSiKAN/Gomb-Soma) + the holonomy-feedback learning rule are the NEXT layers, not yet wired
topology is fixed structural geometry (no gradient through it)
components.supersedes: the flat-vector-into-RLS / precision-matrix latent framing (evolvent line, frozen)
components.note: ON-MISSION re-anchor after the evolvent/Cholesky devolution (see feedback-nagare-closed-form-thesis-not-gaps): latent = mesh tensor contracted, no normal equations. Next: nonlinear per-edge map (HSiKAN/Gomb-Soma) on the edge field + entropy/holonomy feedback as the learning rule.

components.name: conv2d
components.purpose: Learned 2-D convolution — the Neocognitron S-cell (spatial feature detector).
components.output_schema: y: (c_out, H', W') flat f32; grads (grad_x, ConvLayer)
known_limitations: naive (no im2col); fine at G<=32, revisit for large maps

components.name: group_pool
components.purpose: Dihedral group-orbit attention pool — the rotation-equivariant Neocognitron C-cell (invariant resp + equivariant orient).
known_limitations: gives LOCAL orientation-invariance, NOT global rotation-invariance (see F-N3-2)
discrete C_n; between-group angles only approx

components.name: sc_block
components.purpose: Stackable S/C block: conv S-cell (C_in->2K) -> bank of K oriented-unit group_pool C-cells -> K-channel invariant resp map. Output shape = next block input, so blocks stack.
components.output_schema: resp: (K, H', W') invariant; composed backward FD-verified through both cells
known_limitations: C-cell is LOAD-BEARING only in compositional / can't-fit regimes (F-N3-1); REDUNDANT under a learnable single block (F-N2b-1)

components.name: oriented_sobel_bank / ScBlock::new_oriented
components.purpose: Oriented Sobel S-cell warm-start (rotated gradient pair per unit). Canonical cold-start fix for a block feeding an entropy pool.
supported_tasks: any ScBlock (c_in=1, 3x3) that stalls from a random cold start
known_limitations: c_in==1, 3x3 only
a prior, not a guarantee — conv stays learnable

components.name: global_entropy_pool
components.purpose: Globally rotation-invariant top: per-channel response-weighted spatial covariance -> [mean, trace, eigen-entropy Hs] (invariant, arrangement-sensitive) + principal-axis angle (equivariant pose readout).
known_limitations: needs an ORIENTED S-cell upstream (cold-start guard F-ENT-2)
validated on controlled 2-shape task; not scaled to clutter
components.note: Recommended top for the rotation-invariant recognition path (mean-top is arrangement-blind, held-out AUROC ~chance).

components.name: soft_argmax
components.purpose: Differentiable keypoint head (integral pose regression / DSNT).

components.name: auroc
components.purpose: Area under ROC (Mann-Whitney U) — canonical binary-eval utility.
known_limitations: binary only
components.supersedes: duplicated `fn auroc` in neocognitron_deep/entropy/aniso (removed); remaining copies in cpml_signed_link/signed_link/cr_edge_encoder flagged for consolidation

components.name: skeleton_hg_refine (NOT YET EXTRACTED)
components.purpose: Residual signed hg_conv refinement of predicted joint coords over a skeleton graph (cycles/signs/scale + shared elin).
supported_tasks: structural coord refinement where recovery is a single-neighbour translation (P3: 2.6 vs 6.9px)
components.observation_schema: coord: (J,2) + skeleton (cycles, signs, scale) + LinearLayer elin
known_limitations: shared elin expresses neighbour-average / single-neighbour-translation ONLY; CANNOT express asymmetric affine loop closure (P4 HURTS: 10.7 vs 7.3px, F-P4-1)
components.extraction_blocked_reason: Not yet canonical: P4 measured that the shared-elin form is wrong for closed loops. The path to CANONICAL is the PER-EDGE TRANSFORM op (each hyperedge owns its map). Extract to a `SkeletonRefine` framework component ONCE the per-edge transform lands, so the canonical version is the correct one, not the limited one.

components.name: EvolventHead
components.purpose: Incremental/online (evolvent) MULTI-OUTPUT readout — exact forgetting recursive-least-squares over a fixed feature basis; C=1 regression, C=n_classes one-hot -> argmax classification (precision P SHARED across outputs, step O(d^2 + d*C)); per-sample closed-form, NO backward sweep; tracks drift via lambda; covariance-windup guard on by default.
components.source_experiment: evolvent E0 (streaming) + E1 (static benchmark, 50 seeds, kato15)
supported_tasks: static supervised regression AND classification (one-pass, no tuning)
components.observation_schema: phi: &[f32] (d features), y: &[f32] (C targets; one-hot for classification)
components.output_schema: predict -> Vec<f32> (C); predict_class -> argmax; update -> per-output residuals; verified vs batch ridge
known_limitations: O(d^2) per update (win is SAMPLE efficiency / one-pass / no-tuning, not per-step FLOPs)
long-drift STREAMING is windup-limited (F-EVO-3); needs directional forgetting
linear-in-features (readout only); MLP still edges it where learned features matter (iris, california)
components.note: DEPLOYABLE for STATIC supervised readouts (F-EVO-4, 50 seeds fixed-RNG: COMPETITIVE with 200-epoch MLP -- wins 2 loses 2 ties 2 -- beats SGD 6/6, 1-pass no-tuning). Streaming/drift still EXPERIMENTAL (F-EVO-3).

components.name: BlockEvolventHead
components.purpose: Block-structured (hyperedge-clique) precision evolvent -- the alternative to the dense O(d^2) PAIRWISE precision. Contiguous feature blocks (one per hyperedge), block-diagonal precision (small b_e x b_e per block) with a SHARED residual/denominator; step is O(sum b_e^2) = O(d*w). Keeps higher-order interactions (the block features can be k-way) at a fraction of the cost.
components.observation_schema: phi: &[f32] grouped into contiguous blocks (block_sizes), y: f32
components.output_schema: predict -> f32; update -> prequential residual; single block == dense EvolventHead exactly
known_limitations: drops cross-block (separator) coupling -> approximate for non-additive cross-hyperedge structure
contiguous blocks only (assumes features grouped by hyperedge)
the higher-order term must be an explicit feature
components.note: F-EVO-5: matches dense-hyperedge R2 0.997 at 20x less precision storage; pairwise precision provably cannot represent the 3-way term. Path to CANONICAL: junction-tree (block-tridiagonal) form + update through hg_message + SBSH width certificate. E4 (InfoEvolventHead) is the EXACT version of this path.

components.name: InfoEvolventHead
components.purpose: Information-form (junction-tree) evolvent precision -- the EXACT sparse alternative to the dense O(d^2) pairwise P. Accumulates J = Phi^T Phi + ridge*I and b = Phi^T y (each update J += phi phi^T touches only phi's support); solves J w = b. EXACT (= dense RLS, keeps ALL cross-hyperedge coupling) but J is SPARSE for local (bounded-width) measurements -> storage O(nnz(J)) = O(d*w). This is the hypergraph<->tensor conjugation: J IS the signed-hypergraph incidence made numerical (nonzero J[i,k] == GMRF conditional dependence edge).
components.source_experiment: evolvent E4 (examples/evolvent_junction.rs, kato15 5 seeds x 4 n)
supported_tasks: exact online least-squares readout with LOCAL/bounded-width measurements
GMRF / junction-tree precision at O(d*w) storage
the exact form of the E3 hyperedge-clique precision (keeps separator coupling BlockEvolventHead drops)
components.observation_schema: phi: &[f32] (d features, locally supported), y: f32
components.output_schema: solve -> Vec<f32> w (== dense RLS solution exactly); predict(phi,w) -> f32; nnz -> info-matrix sparsity
known_limitations: sparsity is a property of the DATA (local measurements) not the head: dense/global measurements fill J -> no advantage over dense O(d^2)
current solve is dense Gaussian elimination O(d^3); the O(d*w^2) block-tridiagonal Cholesky that exploits nnz is NOT yet implemented (nnz() accounts the sparsity a sparse solve would use)
single-output (regression); linear-in-features (higher-order terms must be explicit features)
measures STORAGE + EXACTNESS win, not yet a solve-TIME win
components.note: F-EVO-6: INFO R2 == DENSE R2 exactly at every (n,seed); info-matrix storage 21.6%->2.8% of dense as n 48->384 (O(d*w) vs O(d^2), halves per doubling). Classical primitive (information filter / Lauritzen-Spiegelhalter junction tree) -- framework value is the exact+sparse evolvent head + hypergraph conjugation, NOT algorithmic novelty. Path to a solve-time win: block-tridiagonal Cholesky + SBSH width certificate. DELIVERED in E5 (JunctionTreeCholesky).

components.name: JunctionTreeCholesky
components.purpose: Multifrontal (clique-tree) Cholesky over a bounded-width SPD information matrix -- the SPARSE SOLVE for the E4 information form. The hypergraph clique tree drives the factorization: each clique is a hyperedge (tensor block), leaves->root elimination sends the Schur complement over each separator up to the parent (the message U = F_SS - F_SR F_RR^-1 F_RS = a contraction over eliminated interior onto the separator boundary, structurally the hg_message edge->node incidence contraction); root->leaves back-substitution. EXACT (= dense Cholesky of the assembled J) at storage O(sum|C|^2)=O(d*w^2) and factorization O(sum|C|^3)=O(d*w^3), vs dense O(d^2)/O(d^3). An online update touches ONLY its clique's path to the root (proven) -> incremental re-fire O(log N * w^3).
components.source_experiment: evolvent E5 (examples/evolvent_multifrontal.rs, kato15 5 seeds x 4 depths)
supported_tasks: exact sparse solve of a bounded-width (junction-tree) SPD precision
incremental/online least-squares over a clique tree with log-depth locality
measuring what cross-clique (separator) coupling is worth (solve_block_diagonal baseline)
components.observation_schema: cliques: Vec<Clique{vars residual-first, n_res, parent}> (valid clique tree, RIP, separator subset of parent, SPD J); update(clique, phi_local, y)
components.output_schema: solve -> Vec<f32> x (== dense Cholesky of assembled J exactly); solve_block_diagonal -> separator-dropping baseline; factor_storage/factor_flops -> O(d*w^2)/O(d*w^3) accounting; ancestors_inclusive -> online-update locality
known_limitations: MEASURED wall-clock speedup (criterion) is 16.7x->682x over d 105->889 AFTER the contiguous-storage rewrite (E10/F-EVO-12); was 5.9x-255x before (E9/F-EVO-11). Flop/measured gap closed 5.0x->1.86x (large d); at d=105 essentially AT the flop ceiling (1.08x). Cite 682x as deployable.
the 682x is vs a Gaussian-elim dense baseline (~2x flops of pure Cholesky) -> layout-independent number is the 2.7x self-speedup from the rewrite; a Cholesky baseline would show ~2x more residual
remaining headroom below the ceiling: frontal-extraction + triangular solves not blocked/SIMD-tuned (not pursued)
incremental re-fire is PROVEN-local but the optimized refactorize_path (recompute only the path) is NOT built (E5 measures the locality that justifies it)

requires a valid clique tree (RIP + bounded width); a measurement coupling non-adjacent cliques breaks the tree -- SBSH width certificate is the guarantee; representability needs width >= interaction order (E8/F-EVO-10)
single-output, linear-in-features
dense per-clique frontals (fine at bounded width)
components.note: F-EVO-7: MF R2 == DENSE R2 at every (depth,seed); factorization FLOP speedup 18x->1270x as d 105->889 (grows (d/w)^2); online update perturbs EXACTLY the path-to-root (log2(N) cliques). Completes the precision family: dense O(d^2)/O(d^3) -> block O(d*w)/approx -> info O(d*w)/exact-dense-solve -> multifrontal O(d*w^2)/O(d*w^3)/exact. Classical method (Duff-Reid multifrontal); framework value = hypergraph clique tree AS the assembly tree, separators AS messages. WALL-CLOCK (criterion): E9/F-EVO-11 measured 5.9x-255x (Vec-per-clique, ~5x below flops); E10/F-EVO-12 CONTIGUOUS-STORAGE rewrite -> 16.7x-682x (2.7x faster solve, same exact answer), flop/measured gap closed 5x->1.86x. 682x is the deployable number (vs a Gaussian dense baseline).
